data.title: Veradermics Raises $30 Million in Private Pre-Funded Warrant Deal With Suvretta Affiliates
data.description:
Veradermics entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement for a concurrent private placement with entities affiliated with Suvretta Capital. The company sold pre-funded warrants to purchase 300,000 shares at $99.99999 per warrant, with a $0.00001 exercise price, generating approximately $30 million in gross proceeds. The deal closed alongside Veradermics' public offering of 3,843,790 shares at $100.00 per share, with a 30-day option for 576,568 additional shares. The warrants feature a 9.99% beneficial ownership cap, adjustable up to 19.99% with notice.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for private placement of pre-funded warrants
  • Counterparty: Suvretta Capital affiliates
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 29 2026 / May 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until fully exercised
  • Reason: Raise capital alongside public offering

Original SEC Filing: Veradermics, Inc [ MANE ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: InspireMD Withdraws Guidance, Recalls CGuard Prime
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

InspireMD is voluntarily recalling its CGuard Prime 135 cm carotid stent delivery system after determining that the system did not meet performance expectations.

The medical technology company said Friday that the voluntary recall came after the stent system for the prevention of stroke did not meet expectations during carotid artery stenting procedures.

The company is implementing design improvements to the CGuard Prime stent delivery system and expects approval from the Food and Drug Administration by the first half of 2027, the company said.

The company is withdrawing its full-year revenue guidance.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: CBOT wheat futures end higher, hover near two-year highs
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade wheat futures ended higher on Friday, with spread trading helping to keep prices hovering near two-year highs, market analysts said.

  • Concerns over drought damage to the U.S. hard red winter wheat crop had investors hedging their bets ahead of a government crop progress report next week, analysts said.

  • CBOT wheat has been climbing since mid-April on worries about drought threatening wheat yields in the U.S. Plains.

  • CBOT July wheat (WN26) settled up 1 cent at $6.37-3/4 a bushel on Friday, after the most-active contract hit a near two-year high at mid-week.

  • K.C. July wheat (KWN26) ended up 1 cent at $6.94-1/2 per bushel.

  • Minneapolis July spring wheat (MWEN26) settled 1-3/4 cents lower at $7.04 per bushel.

  • Global oil prices fell on Friday, retreating after hitting a four-year high of more than $126 a barrel earlier on Thursday on concerns the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could lead to a protracted Middle East supply disruption that could inflict deeper damage on the global economy.

data.title: CBOT wheat futures firm as concerns over drought impact on crop yields mount
data.description:
Key points:
  • Corn and soybean futures rise on potential planting delays, export demand
  • Drought in US Plains raises concerns over wheat yields; traders monitor crop conditions
  • USDA confirms large corn sales to undisclosed destinations, supporting corn market

By P.J. Huffstutter

Chicago Board of Trade wheat futures ended higher on Friday on spread trading, with prices hovering near two-year highs, as concerns over drought damage to the U.S. hard red winter wheat crop had investors hedging their bets ahead of a government crop progress report next week, market analysts said.

CBOT wheat has been climbing since mid-April on worries about drought threatening wheat yields in the U.S. Plains. The most-active contract settled up 1 cent at $6.37-3/4 a bushel on Friday, and notched a third-straight weekly gain.

"We could be going into a 2012 drought scenario for wheat that we had seen back then in the corn," said Mike Zuzolo, president of Global Commodity Analytics. "We have about 30 days of growth before the plant starts dying on us."

Meanwhile, global oil prices fell on Friday, retreating from hitting a four-year high of more than $126 a barrel earlier on Thursday on concerns the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could lead to a protracted Middle East supply disruption that could inflict deeper damage on the global economy.

"Between the weather, what's happening in crude and the uncertainty in the markets, the trade is hunkering down a bit," Zuzolo said.

CBOT corn ended up 5-1/2 cents at $4.80-1/4 a bushel, and touched a one-year high earlier in the day. Several 2027 corn contracts set fresh contract highs and the December corn (CZ26) cracked $5 per bushel during the trading session.

And soybeans settled up 7-3/4 cents at $12.03-1/4 a bushel, crossing the $12 a bushel mark.

CBOT corn futures rallied on ongoing concerns that a sharp rise in fertilizer costs due to the Iran war will crimp farm output over time, questions about planting progress and signs of steady corn export demand.

On Friday, the U.S. Department of Agriculture confirmed private sales of 148,240 metric tons of U.S. corn to undisclosed destinations, including 78,240 tons for delivery in the 2025/26 marketing year that began on September 1, 2025, and 70,000 tons for 2026/27 delivery.

Weather concerns were also at play in the corn and soybean markets, traders said. Some brokers and market analysts said that their farmer clients in central and eastern parts of the U.S. Corn Belt had not yet started planting, either because it had been too wet or too cold.

CBOT soybean futures rose on a bear spread, even as the most-active July contract (SN26) stayed inside Thursday's trading range. Soybean futures drew support as soyoil futures (0#BO:) set fresh contract highs, buoyed by demand for biodiesel.

Crush margins also gave soybean futures a boost. The Chicago Board of Trade July (SMSIN26-BON26-SN26) board crush, one indication of soy processors' profit margins, was last up 3 cents at $3.29 per bushel of soybeans.

data.title: Livento Group, Inc. FY 2025: Revenue $151.4K, Net loss $(12.3M) — 10-K Summary
data.description:
Livento Group, Inc. reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $151.4K, down from $1.69M a year earlier, and a net loss of $(12.32M) compared with a $(596K) loss in FY 2024, according to its Form 10-K.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $151.4K for FY 2025, down from $1.69M in FY 2024 (decline of $1.54M).
  • Net income: Net loss of $(12.32M) for FY 2025, compared with net loss of $(596K) in FY 2024 (worsened year over year).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Annual software services (Elisee) accounted for most revenue; service revenue peaked in 2023 and declined sharply by 2025.
  • Channel shift: Company is strategically pivoting from real estate into entertainment (BOXO) and AI services, prioritizing movie production and development of Elisee.
  • Brand momentum: BOXO has ramped activity with multiple films in production and a pipeline of projects; initial projects have begun generating revenue.
  • Operational milestones: Management expanded staffing, moved to a larger office, pursued OTCQB/Form 10 listing efforts, and continued AI product development.
  • Forward outlook: Management expects growth in Elisee demand and BOXO monetization via disposals and external investment to support future revenue.

Original SEC Filing: Livento Group, Inc. [ NUGN ] - 10-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Origin Materials Plans to Sell Technology, Wind Down
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Origin Materials said its board has approved a plan to sell its PET cap technology and other remaining assets, and then wind down operations.

As part of the plan, the company will reduce its workforce by about 59%, reducing its annual operating expenses by about $14.0 million. The company said it expects to incur about $2.1 million in connection with the workforce reduction.

John Bissell will step down as chief executive officer, effective immediately, but will continue to serve on the board.

Bissell will receive a severance payment equal to four months of his base salary, which was $300,000 in fiscal 2025. He will be eligible for other benefits, the company said.

Matt Plavan, Origin's chief financial officer and chief operating officer, was named as interim CEO.

The company uses sustainable feedstock to create materials and products which are made from fossil feedstock.

Origin said it plans to call a special meeting of shareholders to seek approval of the plan, and intends to file materials relating to the special meeting with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Origin Materials shares closed the day's regular session at $2.66. Trading in shares was halted after hours. Year to date, the stock has lost 58% of its value.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. to Release Fiscal 2026 Third Quarter Results
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--May 01, 2026--

Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. will issue a press release on Friday, May 8, 2026 before the market opens reporting results for its fiscal third quarter ended March 31, 2026.

The Company generally hosts two earnings conference calls per year, one for its fiscal second quarter and one for its fiscal fourth quarter — which schedule allows for a mid-season update, followed by a full-season review. Accordingly, the Company will not hold an earnings conference call this quarter.

About Madison Square Garden Sports Corp.

Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. (MSG Sports) is a leading professional sports company, with a collection of assets that includes the New York Knicks (NBA) and the New York Rangers (NHL), as well as two development league teams — the Westchester Knicks (NBAGL) and the Hartford Wolf Pack (AHL). MSG Sports also operates a professional sports team performance center — the MSG Training Center in Greenburgh, NY. More information is available at www.msgsports.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260501173567/en/

data.title: S&P 500 Extends Longest Weekly Winning Streak Since Late 2024 — WSJ
data.description:
By Jack Pitcher

Stocks' stellar war-defying run continued on Friday.

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed at fresh records after Iran handed Washington a new proposal for ending the war, offering hints of compromise. The two sides remain far apart, The Wall Street Journal reported, but investors nonetheless took it as an encouraging sign, sending Brent crude oil futures down 2% to $108.17 a barrel.

The S&P 500 closed higher for a fifth consecutive week, the longest streak since 2024, and is now up 14% over the past month. Cease-fire hopes helped kick off the rally, and artificial-intelligence speculation, soaring tech stocks and some positive economic data points have extended it.

"Bears have largely capitulated," said Mark Hackett, chief markets strategist at Nationwide.

Tech led the way again on Friday. A strong earnings report lifted Apple 3.3% higher. The iPhone maker's sales surged, beating Wall Street's expectations, and might have been even better if not for the constrained supply of advanced chips that power its devices, executives said on a call with analysts.

That shortage is fueling a powerful rally in chip and memory stocks this year. Intel was among this week's best performers, adding 21% to extend its year-to-date gain to 170%. Qualcomm and Sandisk also posted double-digit weekly gains.

Analysts have also been pointing to signs of an economic acceleration in companies' first quarter results, which have been broadly strong. New data Friday showed factory activity expanded in the U.S. in April even as price pressures rose from the war.

"Tech is leading the way, but earnings growth is broadening, supported by resilient consumer spending and signs of a cyclical upswing," Ulrike Hoffmann-Burchardi, global head of equities at UBS Global Wealth Management, wrote in a Friday research note.

S&P 500 companies have so far exceeded first quarter earnings estimates in aggregate by 21%, well above the five-year average of 7.3%, according to FactSet. First quarter earnings growth has averaged 27%, the highest level since 2021.

It wasn't all rosy news on Friday. Investors who had been betting on a government-backed deal to save Spirit Airlines are now facing big losses. Shares of Spirit Aviation dropped about 60% over-the-counter to less than 60 cents after Journal reported that the ailing budget airline is preparing to cease operations.

Write to Jack Pitcher at jack.pitcher@wsj.com
